text: Welcome to CPE Residency - Unit 1. Please review the course syllabus and the APC Standards of Practice document posted in Materials. Your first verbatim is due January 19. If you have questions about the verbatim template, office hours are Thursday 3:30-5:00 PM in the Pastoral Care Office.

Good work on your first verbatims. A pattern I am noticing: several of you are moving to fix or reassure before the patient finishes speaking. Your job right now is to stay curious, not comfortable. We will work through this in Thursday's group supervision.

Reminder: Unit 1 competency portfolio is due March 23. Make sure you have incorporated my written feedback on at least your first two verbatims before compiling the PDF. Portfolios submitted without revision notes will be returned for resubmission.

Graded supervised visits must be scheduled with me no later than the posted due date for the graded visit - contact me by email to book a slot. I have availability Monday through Thursday 9:00 AM-1:00 PM. These visits take approximately 90 minutes including debrief. Plan accordingly.

Welcome to Unit 2 - Chaplaincy in Specialized Settings. This unit will take you deeper into oncology, ICU, and palliative care ministry. If you have not yet completed Unit 1 portfolio with final feedback incorporated, do not proceed to Unit 2 clinical rotations.

The oncology floor has been particularly active this week. Both of you handled difficult conversations with real presence and maturity - I noticed. Keep writing down what you observed in your field notes immediately after each encounter, while it is still fresh.

Reminder: Unit 2 competency portfolio is due on the posted assignment date. This is also your final CPE submission before the board exam prep period. Dr. Lorraine Vickers will co-review both portfolios. Start your integrative essay early - it requires honest self-assessment, not a highlight reel.

Welcome to Morning Reflections - Show Production Practicum. This course runs alongside the live broadcast season. Key dates: Wed evenings are guest-booking and segment-planning; Thu evenings are script-writing and playlist review. Sat mornings I am live from 7-9 AM - you are welcome to observe from the production room with TJ's approval.

Great work on the devotional scripts. Alicia, yours had a strong opening image - tighten the transition into the scripture and it will land. Jerome, you are writing for the page. Radio is different. Read it out loud before you submit. If it trips you up, it will trip the listener up.

Audacity files and the raw recording sample for cw_305 are now posted in Materials. Use the drive link - do not email me the audio files, they are too large. Export as MP3 320kbps and share the file via Drive before submitting. Questions go to TJ Jackson first, then to me.

Welcome to Congregational Care Training. This is a nine-month program meeting the first Sunday of each month after service. Our goal is to equip each of you to make confident, compassionate visits - whether someone is at home recovering, in the hospital, or grieving a loss. Deacon Mosley and I are both here to support you.

Reminder: November session is November 2 after the 8 AM service. We will be covering grief support and when to refer someone for professional counseling. Bring your boundaries reflection worksheet - we will use it as a discussion prompt. Light breakfast provided.

This is the final assignment of our 2025-2026 program year. Thank you all for your commitment - you have grown tremendously. Boundaries plans are due December 14. Certificates of completion will be presented at the January 4 service. If you have not submitted all three prior assignments, please reach out to me before December 20.

This is the archived workspace for Fall 2025 CPE Residency. Both residents have completed the unit. Portfolios and supervisor evaluations are filed in Materials for reference. If you need documentation for your APC board application, contact Dr. Lorraine Vickers directly at lorraine.vickers@gmail.com.

Reminder: our next Congregational Care Training session covers hospital visitation etiquette and confidentiality. Please review the bedside-presence handout in Materials before we meet. Bring one question from your own visits.
Unit 1 cohort: your mid-unit reflection papers are due before our next group session. Focus on a single pastoral encounter and what it taught you about presence. Two pages, no citations needed.
